examples.text: The sun was sinking down over the sea, the mountain wall with all its clefts and promontories wore a cloak of many colours, when we saw before us on a rock a ruined tower.

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic. Compare Aramaic חֲרוֹב (“be laid waste”), Classical Syriac ܚܪܒ (“be laid waste”), Hebrew חָרַב (kharáv, “to be destroyed”).
